description: Add the ability to forward a message to an external user via email address and provide an option to automatically generate a draft of the message to include when forwarding.
title: Forward message via email with draft generation

descriptionHistory.description: Add the ability to forward a message to an external user via email address and provide an option to automatically generate a draft of the message to include when forwarding.

summary:
## Summary: Email Forwarding with AI Draft Generation

### Changes Implemented
- Added `PROMPT_EMAIL_FORWARD_DRAFT` AI prompt for generating contextual forwarding messages
- Added `sendGraphMail()` function to send emails via Microsoft Graph API
- Created `POST /api/emails/[id]/forward` API route that supports:
  - Forwarding Outlook and Gmail emails to any external email address
  - AI-generated forwarding message drafts (via `generateDraft: true` flag)
  - User-written forwarding messages
  - Standard forwarded email formatting with original email headers (From, Date, Subject, To)
  - Proper HTML escaping for user content and metadata fields
- Added Forward button and modal to the email detail page (`/inbox/[id]`)
- Added Forward button (hover action) and modal to the inbox list page (`/inbox`)
- Both modals include:
  - Email context preview (subject, sender)
  - Recipient email input
  - Optional message textarea
  - "AI Generate" button that auto-generates a forwarding message using the AI client
  - Send/Cancel actions with loading states and error handling via toast notifications

### Files Modified
- `lib/ai/prompts.ts` — Added `PROMPT_EMAIL_FORWARD_DRAFT` constant and prompt definition
- `lib/graph/messages.ts` — Added `sendGraphMail()` function for Graph API email sending

### Files Created
- `app/api/emails/[id]/forward/route.ts` — New API route for email forwarding and AI draft generation

### Files Modified (UI)
- `app/(app)/inbox/[id]/page.tsx` — Added forward state, Forward button in header, and Forward modal
- `app/(app)/inbox/page.tsx` — Added forward state, Forward hover action button, and Forward modal

### Notes for Developer
- Forwarding uses the Microsoft Graph API `sendMail` endpoint via client credentials flow (same pattern as email deletion)
- The Graph API app registration requires `Mail.Send` permission for forwarding to work
- Gmail-sourced emails can also be forwarded — they are sent through the user's connected Outlook account via Graph API
- The Outlook OAuth scopes include `Mail.ReadWrite` but not explicitly `Mail.Send` — the app-level credentials (`.default` scope) should include `Mail.Send` if configured in Azure AD
- The AI draft generation uses the `PROMPT_EMAIL_FORWARD_DRAFT` prompt which can be customized per user via the admin prompts UI
